title: fix(artifact): show :latest tag in ls output when no explicit tag given
body:
## Description

Fixes #27083

When an artifact is added without an explicit tag (e.g. `quay.io/myimage/myartifact`), the `TAG` column in `podman artifact ls` was empty instead of showing `latest`, unlike container images which default to `:latest`.

## Fix

Add a call to `reference.TagNameOnly()` after parsing the stored artifact name in `outputTemplate`, so the reference is normalised before the tag is extracted. `reference.TagNameOnly` is already imported and used elsewhere in the codebase — this is a one-line display-side fix consistent with how `NewArtifactReference` already normalises on the storage side.

```go
// Before
named, ok := repo.(reference.Named)
...
if tagged, ok := named.(reference.Tagged); ok {
    tag = tagged.Tag()
}
// tag == "" for untagged artifacts → empty TAG column

// After
named = reference.TagNameOnly(named) // normalise: adds :latest if no tag present
if tagged, ok := named.(reference.Tagged); ok {
    tag = tagged.Tag()
}
// tag == "latest" ✓
```

## Testing

```bash
podman artifact add quay.io/myimage/myartifact /tmp/file.txt
podman artifact ls
# REPOSITORY                    TAG     DIGEST         CREATED       SIZE
# quay.io/myimage/myartifact    latest  sha256:abc...  1 minute ago  1.2 kB
```

comments.body: cc @baude @rhatdan @mheon - you have recently worked on the artifact area, would appreciate a review when you get a chance.
[NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.
do look into it

The failing `sys podman rawhide rootless host` check (`119 |060| podman mount no-dereference`) is unrelated to this PR - it exercises `podman mount`, not `podman artifact`, and does not touch any code path modified here. This appears to be a pre-existing flaky test or infrastructure issue on the CI host.

@mheon could you approve the workflow so the full CI can run?

Quick summary of what this PR does: when a user runs `podman artifact add` without an explicit tag (e.g. `podman artifact add localhost/myapp /path/to/file`), the `TAG` column in `podman artifact ls` was showing blank instead of `latest`. The fix normalizes the reference using `reference.TagNameOnly()` before listing, so untagged artifacts consistently show `latest`, matching the behaviour users would expect from Docker/OCI conventions.

Also included an e2e test in `test/e2e/artifact_test.go` to prevent regressions.

The two currently failing checks (`sys podman rawhide rootless host` and `testing-farm:fedora-43-x86_64:podman-fedora`) both fail on `podman mount no-dereference` which is unrelated to artifact listing. Happy to provide more context if needed.
@containers/podman-maintainers PTAL
LGTM
Thanks @DeveshB-1
The same approach was _rejected_ in #28149, and there is no recorded discussion about why it is <s>not</s> now acceptable. What do we want to do? Revert and re-review?
Concur with a revert. Sorry for LGTMing, wasn't aware of earlier discussion.

@mtrmac @mheon - thanks for the context, I wasn't aware of the earlier rejection.

I understand the concern: fixing this at display time means `podman artifact ls` shows `:latest` but other operations (push, inspect, etc.) still operate on the untagged reference, which is inconsistent.

The correct fix would be to normalize the reference at **storage time** - apply `reference.TagNameOnly()` in `NewArtifactReference` (or wherever the name is parsed during `podman artifact add`), so `:latest` is stored from the start and all operations see it consistently.

Happy to open a follow-up PR with that approach. If you'd prefer to revert this one first, that's fine too - just let me know.

I do think that _if_ we want artifacts’s names to always include a tag or digest, that should be enforced when creating those names. I don’t at this point have an opinion on where exactly that should happen.

@mtrmac agreed - the right place is `NewArtifactReference` in `pkg/domain/infra/abi/artifact.go`, where the name string is first parsed into a reference. Applying `reference.TagNameOnly()` there would normalize `:latest` at creation time, so all subsequent operations (add, ls, push, inspect) see a consistent reference.

I can open a follow-up PR with that fix and revert this one if that's the preferred path.
plrease revert this.  also, if you have something AI agentish that is doing all these responses, please consider changing your behavior because that is in part what is causing confusion.

… also, _now that we have users that have untagged references in stores_, do we need to worry about managing such stores if we disable repo-only references?

It would be natural to normalize repo-only references using `TagNameOnly`, but we might end up with a store that contains an artifact that can’t be deleted.
fair point @baude I have been using AI tooling to help with some of the responses and commits. I understand the code and the fix, and I'll be more deliberate going forward
should i revert the pr ?
Yes, I would appreciate a PR that reverts this and proposes a cleaner fix. (I don’t have a specific clean fix in mind, that would need to be figured out.)
got it
Revert in https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/28459
